exemplar.title: Optimization of Paper Packaging Material to Reduce Waste

detailedExemplarData.title: Optimization of Paper Packaging Material to Reduce Waste

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear investigative path with logical progression from introduction through applications to conclusion
Wellâdefined structure with title page, table of contents, labelled sections, and distinct introduction/body/conclusion
Mostly relevant content with limited repetition and mostly concise presentation
weaknesses: Inconsistent table of contents formatting and truncated entries reduce navigational clarity
Occasional duplicated headings and incomplete sentences interrupt coherence
Citation formatting and stray OCR errors distract from overall polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with a clear structure and mostly relevant content (band 3). Minor lapsesâsuch as formatting inconsistencies in the table of contents, stray headings, and occasional incomplete sentencesâprevent it from reaching the highest level of organizational excellence. A score of 3 reflects solid presentation with room for refinement in formatting and proofreading.

strengths: Consistent use of mathematical notation (Ï, r, h, derivatives) throughout the work
Effective use of algebraic expressions, labelled diagrams, and a bar graph to support the analysis
Good logical flow in explanations, with step-by-step justification of calculus procedures
weaknesses: Minor notation inconsistencies (variable capitalization, mis-rendered fractions) hinder full precision
Dense blocks of algebra in the cone and quotient-rule derivations reduce clarity
Graphical representations (bar graph) lack axis labels and units
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is generally clear, with mostly consistent notation and effective multiple representations supporting the argument (band 3). Occasional cluttered derivations, notation slips, and missing axis details prevent exceptional clarity. Awarding 3 reflects strong overall communication with specific areas for improvement in layout and precision.

strengths: Investigation demonstrates significant independent thinking through collection and analysis of real packaging
Clear personal approach linking interests in sustainability and accounting to the mathematical exploration
Good creativity in selecting everyday objects (e.g., pizza box) for optimisation applications
weaknesses: Mathematical methodology follows standard routines without novel modelling techniques
Narrative occasionally drifts into generic commentary, limiting the sense of a highly innovative personal approach
Use of technology and original extensions is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows clear personal engagement and independent thinking beyond textbook examples, applying mathematics to personally relevant real-life contexts (band 2). While the work is creative and motivated, it lacks the exceptional originality or innovative methods needed for the highest level of personal engagement.

strengths: Thoughtful analysis of waste percentages and environmental implications throughout
Meaningful reflection on methodological limitations, such as ruler-based measurement error
Integration of reflection into evaluation with sensible commentary on generalisability
weaknesses: Evaluation sometimes focuses broadly on environmental issues rather than tying insights directly to the research question
Reflection remains descriptive and does not delve into deeper ethical or mathematical implications
Limited quantitative linking of new ideas (marketing rationales) back to optimisation results
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration includes meaningful reflection on results and methodology, with thoughtful analysis and some consideration of future improvements (band 2). However, the reflection lacks the depth and critical insight required for the top band. A score of 2 accurately captures the level of reflective engagement with room to deepen the critique.

strengths: Relevant calculus, algebra, and geometric formulae applied effectively to optimise waste across three contexts
Mathematics remains fully at SL course level, demonstrating purposeful selection of methods
Good conceptual understanding with correct identification of optimal conditions (e.g., h = 2r)
weaknesses: Minor algebraic inconsistencies and typographical errors in derivations (e.g., cone and prism calculations)
Inconsistent significant-figure usage detracts from mathematical precision
Final expressions contain small misplacements under the square root requiring verification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies relevant mathematics competently and at the appropriate level, showing good understanding despite minor errors (aligned with band 3). While the calculus and algebra are purposeful and mostly correct, the presence of algebraic slipups and inconsistent precision prevent a higher mark. A score of 3 reflects solid use of mathematics with targeted areas for correction.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations are generally step-by-step, with justification of each calculus procedure and links back to the investigation aim.  A few derivations (especially for the cone) become cluttered and include apparent algebraic errors, reducing clarity.  Nevertheless, the logical structure is good and communication is clear overall, warranting band 3.

The student shows good conceptual understanding, explaining each calculus step and interpreting results.  Minor algebraic inconsistencies (e.g., cone derivative simplification, miscopied quadratic expression for h) indicate some loss of accuracy, but the majority of results are correct and sensible.  This corresponds to âgood understanding with minor errorsâ (band 3).

The student employs algebraic expressions, labelled diagrams of each solid, a photograph of each package and a bar graph summarising results.  These different representations clearly support the discussion, though dynamic technology (e.g. spreadsheet screenshots of calculations) is absent.  Effectiveness is good but not outstanding, so band 3 is appropriate.

The exploration follows a clear investigative path: introduction â general optimisation proofs â three real-life applications â evaluation â conclusion.  Arguments flow logically from the calculus derivations to the waste calculations and then to environmental discussion, so the reader seldom loses the thread.  
Minor lapses such as duplicated headings and occasional clutter from screen-grabbed algebra add some noise, preventing the work from reaching the âhighly coherentâ standard, but overall coherence and logical development are solid.

Selecting three everyday packages and deriving each optimisation formula demonstrates creativity and initiative at a good level.  Yet no original modelling techniques or advanced software are employed, and the cone derivation largely mirrors online sources.  This fits a âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ level.

There is thoughtful analysis of results â e.g., linking higher waste in prisms to overlapping flaps.  Nevertheless, the analysis does not rigorously test sensitivity to rounding or alternative models, so insight stays at a âthoughtfulâ rather than âdeep and insightfulâ level (band 2).

Outcomes are evaluated in terms of percentage waste and environmental impact, with some commentary on practical constraints.  Future improvements are mentioned briefly, but a comprehensive evaluation with quantitative validation is absent.  Hence, band 2 is suitable.

The student reflects on measurement error, practicality of resized packaging and generalisability, showing meaningful reflection integrated into the body and the evaluation section.  The reflection, however, remains descriptive and does not probe deeper ethical or mathematical implications, placing it in band 2.

Mathematical notation (Ï, r, h, derivatives, â) is generally correct and used in almost every line.  Occasional slips such as inconsistent capitalisation of variables and mis-rendered fractions are present but do not impede understanding.  This corresponds to âmostly consistentâ usage (band 3).

The investigation is clearly driven by the studentâs personal interest in business sustainability; the linkage to corporate social responsibility and accounting shows a personal approach.  Still, methods remain conventional and the narrative occasionally drifts into generic commentary, so the approach is âclearâ but not âhighly innovativeâ (band 2).

Most passages are relevant to the aim; unnecessary material is limited to a few duplicated derivative lines and lengthy formula screenshots.  The student occasionally restates definitions (e.g. conditions for maxima/minima) and repeats working, so the write-up is not fully concise.  Overall it is concise with mostly relevant content, aligned with band 3.

All mathematics used (calculus optimisation, algebraic manipulation, geometric formulae) is directly relevant to minimising material or maximising volume.  The approach is applied effectively to three contexts, demonstrating purposeful selection and correct deployment; this aligns with the ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ descriptor (band 4).

The choice to collect real packaging, create general optimisation formulas and compute percentage waste indicates significant independent thinking beyond textbook examples.  However, the mathematics itself follows standard optimisation routines and does not break new conceptual ground, so the evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

The IA contains a title page, table of contents, labelled sections, and a distinct introduction, body, evaluation and conclusion.  Figures are numbered and referred to in the text, and each real-life example is clearly introduced and closed.  
However, the table of contents formatting is flawed and some pages include partial or repeated headings.  These small organisational weaknesses place it below the âexceptionally well organisedâ level.

Differential calculus and geometric reasoning are fully within the SL syllabus and are applied in non-trivial contexts.  No HL-level or below-standard material dominates.  Therefore, the mathematics is âfully commensurate with course levelâ, meriting band 4.

annotations.comment: The bar graph is a useful representation but lacks axis labels and units, reducing interpretability. Add clear axis titles and units.
The evaluation discussion becomes broad, focusing on environmental impacts rather than explicitly tying findings back to the research question; tighten the analysis accordingly.
Acknowledging ruler-based measurement error as a limitation demonstrates meaningful reflection on methodological assumptions.
Noting limited generalizability is valid but could be enriched by proposing data-collection across multiple products; suggest expanding the sample.
The conclusion introduces new marketing rationales but lacks quantitative linkage to the optimized surface-area results; reinforce with numerical insights.
The title is clear and descriptive, immediately conveying the investigationâs focus on optimizing packaging waste, which supports reader orientation.
The table of contents displays inconsistent formatting and truncated entries (e.g., broken symbols), reducing navigational clarity. Consider reformatting entries for uniformity.
The methodology section ends mid-sentence, suggesting incomplete description of materials or procedures. Complete this section to clarify all steps taken.
The student links personal interests in accounting and sustainability with the mathematical investigation, demonstrating clear personal engagement and motivation.
When introducing the cylinderâs surface area formula, define each variable (r for radius, h for height) explicitly to enhance clarity.
The phrase âbecause h the common variableâ omits a verb, hindering readability. Proofread for grammatical accuracy to improve coherence.
The differentiation steps are correct but presented in a dense block. Separate and align each derivative step clearly to improve mathematical communication.
The reflection on switching from the number line to the second derivative test shows thoughtful methodological consideration and efficiency.
The relationship h=2r is clearly articulated as the optimal condition, effectively linking the calculus result back to the physical dimensions.
Include an uncertainty estimate for ruler-based measurements to acknowledge measurement error and strengthen the evaluation of results.
The cone surface area derivation is presented in one dense block, with some algebraic misplacements. Break it into intermediate steps and verify each simplification.
The quotient rule application spans many lines and repeats steps. Simplify the presentation and ensure each derivative is correctly applied for clarity.
Explicitly state that each term under the second derivative is positive, and thus the sum exceeds zero, to strengthen your logical justification.
Significant figures are inconsistently applied in the cone calculations (e.g., 15.207âcm). Standardize rounding to three significant figures across all measurements.
The net diagram of the rectangular prism is clear and supports the derivation of volume. Effective use of graphical representation.
The quadratic discriminant simplification in the rectangular prism derivation omits proper expansion of bÂ². Revisit the algebra to ensure correct expression.
The final expression for h contains a misplacement under the square root. Verify the derivation steps to correct this typographical error.
The pizza box application demonstrates creativity and a personal approach by linking real packaging to the optimization formulas.
When applying the optimal h formula to the pizza box, include numerical evaluation of the square root to clarify intermediate steps.
Citation entries vary in format and punctuation; apply a consistent style guide to enhance presentation and academic rigor.
Ensure URLs in the works cited are consistently formatted and clickable where appropriate to maintain accessibility and accuracy.
The stray heading ââ¼ onel usionâ in the table of contents appears to be an OCR or formatting error; remove or correct it for coherence.
